title: thymus glabrescens subsp. urumovii
intro: Thymus glabrescens subsp. urumovii, also known as Urumov's thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, belonging to the Lamiaceae family and native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Urumov's thyme include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Urumov's thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
